payload.title: Check for terminating namespace before restarting deployment
payload.description:
laconic-so deployment restart fails with 403 Forbidden if the target
namespace is in Terminating state (from a previous restart). Cryovial should
check namespace status before calling restart and wait for termination to
complete.

Error: unable to create new content in namespace laconic-laconic-<id>
because it is being terminated

Fix: before calling laconic-so restart, run kubectl get ns <namespace> and
check if status.phase is Terminating. If so, wait (with timeout) for it to
disappear, then proceed.

payload.title: GHCR auth: k8s cluster needs imagePullSecret for private images
payload.description: Pods fail with ImagePullBackOff/401 Unauthorized when pulling from ghcr.io/gorbagana-dev/. The k8s cluster has no imagePullSecret for GHCR. Previous deploys used kind load to sidestep this. With imagePullPolicy: Always the cluster needs GHCR credentials. Options: create imagePullSecret from ghcr_token, or make packages public.

payload.resolution: Not a cryovial issue. Moved to wd-h4h and wd-f2f in woodburn_deployer — laconic-so needs REGISTRY_TOKEN in config.env to create imagePullSecrets.

payload.title: Deploy ignores image from webhook — uses :latest tag causing no-op deploys
payload.description:
CI pushes SHA-tagged images (e.g. dumpster-backend:8806996...) and sends the tag in the webhook payload. But deploy() ignores the image field and calls laconic-so deployment restart with no image argument. laconic-so patches the Deployment with the same :latest tag. k8s sees no diff, no rolling update, no image re-pull.

Fix: deploy() accepts image param from webhook. Uses kubectl to patch the k8s Deployment container image directly with the SHA tag. k8s sees different tag → rolling update.

Files: src/cryovial/deploy.py (accept image, kubectl patch), src/cryovial/server.py (pass image from payload to deploy).

payload.title: No deploy records — failures are invisible
payload.description:
Cryovial is fire-and-forget. No deploy records, no way to query status. Deploy failures are invisible — daemon thread catches exception, logs it, but logs go to stdout of a manually-started process.

Fix: Add DeployRecord dataclass. Write YAML file per deploy to ~/.cryovial/deploys/ with id, service, image, status (accepted/completed/failed), timestamps, error. Write record on accept, update on completion/failure.

Files: src/cryovial/deploy.py (DeployRecord, write records).

payload.title: No concurrency control — overlapping deploys on rapid webhooks
payload.description:
Two webhooks arriving close together (frontend + backend CI finish simultaneously) spawn two deploy threads that overlap on the same deployment directory. Race conditions on laconic-so commands.

Fix: Per-deployment cooldown. Track last deploy timestamp per stack_name. If a deploy for the same stack_name completed within 5 minutes, skip (return 429 Too Many Requests). Simple, no locks or queues.

Files: src/cryovial/server.py (track timestamps, check cooldown in do_POST).

payload.title: Artifact download fails on private repos — no GitHub App auth on HTTP request
payload.description:
When deploy_type is artifact and the repo is private, cryovial downloads the release asset via urllib without authentication. GitHub returns 404 for private repo assets without a token.

Cryovial has GITHUB_APP_ID/PEM configured but the artifact download path does not generate an installation token or attach an Authorization header to the download request.

Reproduced: mark-2-marquette/sniper (private) → release build-20260410-192446-ff2e77c → cryovial gets 404 on sniper-x86_64-linux asset URL.

Fix: Before downloading, generate a GitHub App installation token (POST /app/installations/{id}/access_tokens with JWT), then pass Authorization: token <tok> header on the asset download request. The GitHub API returns a redirect to a signed S3 URL — follow it.
